source.multipart_basis: Decomposition inferred from a RoboMaster RMUC 2025 Standard Robot 17 mm flywheel launcher architecture (barrel + feeder + 2 drive motor mounts + gimbal yaw/pitch plates + breech + hopper + turret mounting plate). No rulebook citations added beyond those carried by the original sample.

full_prompt:
Design a 17 mm projectile launcher assembly for a RoboMaster RMUC 2025 Standard Robot as a multi-part bolted assembly rather than a single-body machined housing. The launcher uses two counter-rotating flywheels to fire 17 mm polycarbonate BBs (nominal projectile diameter 16.8 +/- 0.2 mm, nominal mass 3.2 g) at a muzzle velocity limited by RMUC 2025 Rules v1.1.0 to 30 m/s for the Standard class with a heat cap. The launcher is one sub-assembly within the robot's 17 mm turret; total chassis mass budget for the Standard class is 20 kg maximum. The launcher assembly envelope must fit within 150 mm (length, along bore axis) x 80 mm (width) x 80 mm (height). The assembly consists of: a 17.5 mm ID x 80 mm smooth-bore barrel tube; a feeder magazine housing presenting BBs single-file to the flywheels; two drive motor mounts for the 3508 brushless motors at 32 mm center spacing; a gimbal yaw plate and pitch plate carrying the launcher on the 2-axis gimbal; a breech block at the rear of the barrel feeding the BB into the flywheel gap; an ammo hopper above the feeder; and the 4-bolt (M4, 40 mm square) turret mounting plate. Housing parts are 6061-T6 aluminium (E 69 GPa, nu 0.33, density 2700 kg/m^3, yield 276 MPa, UTS 310 MPa). Flywheels are 2024-T3 aluminium (73.1 GPa, 0.33, 2780 kg/m^3, yield 345 MPa, UTS 483 MPa). Load cases: LC1 (Shot reaction) impulsive 1 kN bore-axis reaction over 5 ms at barrel root with turret interface fully fixed; LC2 (Flywheel spin) 10,000 rpm steady rotation of each 60 mm flywheel (~2.8 kN rim centrifugal); LC3 (Barrel heat soak) uniform 40 K rise across the barrel with housing bolts fixed; LC4 (Turret crash) 20 g lateral shock with turret bolts fixed. Pass/fail: (R1) housing max von Mises LC1 <= 184 MPa; (R2) flywheel LC2 <= 230 MPa; (R3) flywheel radial growth LC2 <= 0.15 mm; (R4) combined LC1+LC3 housing peak <= 200 MPa; (R5) housing LC4 <= 184 MPa; (R6) total assembly mass <= 600 g; (R7) barrel ID under LC3 in [17.0, 18.0] mm. Primary verification: linear static FEA for LC1, LC2, LC4; thermal-structural coupled for LC3 and combined LC1+LC3; centrifugal for LC2. Modal, CFD, radiation not required. Deliverables: STEP AP242 assembly + report (mass, von Mises per case, barrel ID change, radial growth).

--- ASSEMBLY RECONFIGURATION ---
The launcher is decomposed into: (A) barrel tube 17.5 mm ID x 80 mm length (6061-T6 with bore finish); (B) feeder magazine housing presenting BBs single-file ahead of the flywheel gap; (C) two drive motor mounts bolted to the gimbal pitch plate at 32 mm center spacing carrying the 3508 motors; (D) gimbal yaw plate (turret yaw rotation interface); (E) gimbal pitch plate (launcher pitch interface); (F) breech block at the rear of the barrel feeding the BB from the feeder into the flywheel gap; (G) ammo hopper above the feeder (gravity-fed BB reservoir); (H) turret mounting plate with 4x M4 on 40 mm square pattern; (I) M3/M4 fastener set. Flywheels and motors are external and modelled as mass/inertia only. Interfaces: IF1 barrel threaded into breech block; IF2 feeder bolted to breech block; IF3 motor mounts bolted to pitch plate; IF4 pitch plate pinned to yaw plate (axis bearings); IF5 yaw plate bolted to turret mounting plate; IF6 hopper bolted to feeder. FEM verification runs on the ASSEMBLED multi-body model only — per-part stress is reported but does not gate pass/fail. Inter-part connection DCR must be <= 1.0 under the governing load case (LC1 combined with LC3). All inherited load magnitudes, material properties, and pass/fail thresholds from the original single-part brief are preserved verbatim; assembly-scope criteria are added.
short_prompt:
Design a 17 mm projectile launcher assembly for a RoboMaster RMUC 2025 Standard Robot as a multi-part bolted assembly. Two counter-rotating flywheels fire 17 mm polycarbonate BBs (diameter 16.8 +/- 0.2 mm, mass 3.2 g) at muzzle velocity limited by RMUC 2025 Rules v1.1.0 to 30 m/s Standard class with heat cap. Chassis mass budget 20 kg max.

Envelope: 150 mm (along bore) x 80 mm x 80 mm. Barrel 17.5 mm ID x 80 mm; 3508 motors at 32 mm center spacing; turret mount 4x M4 on 40 mm square. Housings: 6061-T6 (yield 276 MPa, UTS 310 MPa). Flywheels: 2024-T3 (yield 345 MPa, UTS 483 MPa).

Load cases: LC1 shot reaction 1 kN over 5 ms at barrel root; LC2 flywheel spin 10,000 rpm on 60 mm flywheels (approximately 2.8 kN rim centrifugal); LC3 barrel heat soak 40 K uniform rise; LC4 turret crash 20 g lateral.

Pass/fail: (R1) housing LC1 <= 184 MPa; (R2) flywheel LC2 <= 230 MPa; (R3) flywheel radial growth LC2 <= 0.15 mm; (R4) LC1+LC3 housing peak <= 200 MPa; (R5) housing LC4 <= 184 MPa; (R6) mass <= 600 g; (R7) barrel ID under LC3 in [17.0, 18.0] mm.

prompt.title: RoboMaster RMUC 2025 17 mm flywheel launcher (multi-part bolted assembly)
prompt.brief: 17 mm flywheel launcher: 6061-T6 barrel tube + feeder magazine + 2 drive-motor mounts + gimbal yaw plate + pitch plate + breech block + ammo hopper + turret mounting plate, within 150 x 80 x 80 mm envelope. FEM verification at assembled level only; connection DCR <= 1.0 under governing LC1+LC3 combined case.

boundary_conditions.mounting: turret mounting plate fully fixed for LC1, LC3, LC4; flywheel axis pinned for LC2
boundary_conditions.loads: bore-axis impulse, centrifugal on flywheel, uniform dT on barrel, or lateral shock

parts.name: barrel_tube
parts.role: 17.5 mm ID smooth-bore barrel tube guiding the BB out the muzzle

parts.name: feeder_magazine_housing
parts.role: presents BBs single-file from hopper to the flywheel gap

parts.name: drive_motor_mount
parts.role: bolts M3508 brushless motor to pitch plate with bearing alignment (2x, 32 mm center spacing)

parts.name: gimbal_yaw_plate
parts.role: yaw-axis gimbal plate carrying the pitch yoke

parts.name: gimbal_pitch_plate
parts.role: pitch-axis gimbal plate carrying the motor mounts, barrel, feeder, and breech

parts.name: breech_block
parts.role: rear of the barrel feeding the BB from the feeder into the flywheel gap, carries LC1 impulsive reaction

parts.name: ammo_hopper
parts.role: gravity-fed BB reservoir feeding the feeder (holds ~50 BB)

parts.name: turret_mounting_plate
parts.role: 4x M4 mounting plate to turret yoke (40 mm square pattern)

parts.name: fastener_set
parts.role: M3/M4 bolts tying barrel-breech, feeder, motor mounts, pitch/yaw plates, and hopper

assembly_envelope.overall_bbox: 150 x 80 x 80 mm along bore axis / width / height

pass_fail_criteria.description: Connection demand-to-capacity ratio (DCR) across all inter-part interfaces <= 1.0 under the governing load case (LC1 shot reaction combined with LC3 thermal soak).

interfaces.mediated_by: threaded (M20 x 1.5 barrel thread into breech)
interfaces.load_path: LC1 bore-axis impulsive reaction from barrel into breech block
BB-feed drag and feeder reaction into breech block
flywheel torque and centrifugal bearing reaction into pitch plate
pitch moments from launcher mass into yaw plate
launcher inertial / LC4 crash load into turret yoke

fem_check_policy.rationale: Multipart entries are verified on the assembled model. The LC1 impulsive bore-axis reaction crosses barrel-to-breech thread, feeder-to-breech bolts, and the gimbal pitch/yaw chain back to the turret mount; assembly-level DCR is required, per-part stress is reported but does not gate pass/fail.

notes.why_multi_part: A RoboMaster flywheel launcher of this class is built from a bolted assembly of barrel + breech + feeder + motor mounts + gimbal yaw/pitch plates + hopper rather than a single monolithic housing; the LC1 shot reaction and LC4 crash shock propagate through multiple bolted joints.
notes.speculation_caveats: Count of 2 motor mounts, inclusion of yaw/pitch plates and ammo hopper, and fastener sizing are programmatic speculation consistent with RMUC Standard-class launcher topology; projectile specs, loads, materials, and pass/fail thresholds inherit the original brief verbatim.
